summary_sections.title: Lesson Overview
summary_sections.content_html:
Overview

This lesson covers the fundamental measures of center used to analyze and summarize data sets. You'll learn how to calculate and apply the mean, median, and mode, and understand when each is most appropriate.

summary_sections.title: Definitions and Concepts
summary_sections.content_html:
Key Definitions

What is the definition of 'Normal distribution'?

"Normal distribution" means: A symmetric, bell-shaped probability distribution fully characterized by mean μ (center) and standard deviation σ (spread). The other options describe different concepts.

What is the definition of 'Empirical Rule (68-95-99.7)'?

"Empirical Rule (68-95-99.7)" means: For normal distributions: ~68% of data within ±1σ, ~95% within ±2σ, and ~99.7% within ±3σ of the mean. The other options describe different concepts.

What is the definition of 'Normal probability plot (Q-Q plot)'?

"Normal probability plot (Q-Q plot)" means: A graphical tool that plots ordered data against expected normal quantiles; a roughly linear pattern indicates approximate normality. The other options describe different concepts.

What is the definition of 'Standard normal distribution'?

"Standard normal distribution" means: The normal distribution with mean 0 and standard deviation 1, used as the reference distribution for z-score calculations. The other options describe different concepts.

What is the definition of 'Density curve'?

"Density curve" means: A smooth curve that models the overall shape of a distribution; the total area under the curve equals 1 and represents probability. The other options describe different concepts.

summary_sections.title: Real-World Applications
summary_sections.content_html:
Real-World Applications

Example: Which term is described by: "The normal distribution with mean 0 and standard deviation 1, used as the reference distribution for z-score calculations"?

This description refers to "Standard normal distribution".
